listings.title: Used 2006 Audi S4 quattro AWD 2dr Convertible 6A

truecar.note: TrueCar body-style URL filter did not apply on this run — page returned all body styles. Fallback URL needed; check SETUP.md for alternate param syntax.

top10.introHtml: Ranked for a cheap fun second car near Media, PA (<$15k, ~35-mile radius). Condition and model reliability weighted over raw mileage. Listings pulled 2026-05-13 from Cars.com, CarGurus, and AutoTrader (TrueCar filter non-functional this run).

picks.title: 2006 Mazda MX-5 Miata Touring
picks.whyGood: The NC Miata (2006–2015) is the gold standard cheap fun roadster — a 2.0L inline-4 with near-zero reliability drama, a perfectly balanced chassis, and a soft top that operates with one hand. At 82K miles it has significant life left; NC Miatas routinely go past 200K. No accidents reported. Thompson Lexus is a franchised dealer — likely passed at least a basic lot inspection. The NC uses a timing chain (no belt service needed), so that worry disappears entirely.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the soft top fully — listen for rattle at the header rail, inspect the fabric for tears, check the rear plastic window for yellowing or cracking. Inspect rocker panels and floor for rust (critical in salty states). Ask for service records and confirm clutch engages cleanly. Look for any oil weep at the cam cover.
picks.bottomLine: Best overall pick — a clean, relatively low-mileage Miata at a fair price from a reputable dealer.

picks.title: 2001 BMW 325 Ci
picks.whyGood: The E46 325Ci is arguably the best-driving BMW ever made under $15k. 60,200 miles is exceptional for a 25-year-old car — barely used. Great Deal badge, only 16 miles away, from Mancini Motors which is a known local operation. Silky inline-6, perfect weight balance.
picks.whatToVerify: Oil leaks are the E46's signature fault — check valve cover gasket, rear main seal, and oil pan before buying. Soft top must cycle smoothly with no hydraulic hesitation (check the top ram for seepage). Inspect the rear window for cracking or delamination. Cooling system: water pump, thermostat, and plastic expansion tank all have finite lives at this age.
picks.bottomLine: Rare combination of very low mileage and Great Deal on a legendary E46 convertible — worth a quick drive to Norristown immediately.

picks.title: 2006 Pontiac Solstice Roadster
picks.whyGood: A genuine rear-wheel-drive sports car on the Kappa platform. 63K miles is very low for a 2006, and CarGurus rates it Great Deal at just $7,500. Manual gearbox almost certain — exactly what this car is built for. Simple enough mechanically that any independent shop can maintain it.
picks.whatToVerify: Work the manual soft top through open/close — check the latches and header bow condition. Inspect rear quarter drainage channels (Solstice tops can pool water there and rot the sill). Look for oil leaks at the rear main and valve cover. Verify all HVAC controls work (known weak point). Check under the rear valance for frame surface rust.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-priced genuine sports car on the list with the most life left — Great Deal at 63K miles is very hard to ignore.

picks.title: 2009 Pontiac Solstice GXP Convertible
picks.whyGood: The GXP is the turbocharged Solstice with 260 hp from the 2.0L Ecotec — one of the most undervalued performance convertibles ever sold. Power-to-price ratio is unmatched on this list. Only 18 miles away in Pennsauken. At 88K miles the drivetrain is stout when oil change intervals have been respected.
picks.whatToVerify: Check turbo boost response — should spool cleanly with no hesitation or smoke at full throttle. Inspect intercooler piping for cracks. Ask specifically about oil change history (critical on turbocharged engines). Same soft-top and sill inspection as the base Solstice. No deal badge, so negotiate from knowledge of recent service.
picks.bottomLine: 260 turbocharged horsepower for $8,195 — the most fun-per-dollar on the list if the turbo is healthy.

picks.title: 2000 BMW Z3 2.3 Roadster
picks.location: Selden Motors, Willow Grove PA (23 mi)
picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.3 (with the silky M52 inline-6) is a proper driver’s roadster — RWD, well-balanced, entertaining at any speed. Selden Motors in Willow Grove is a local dealer. 75K miles is modest. Good Deal badge and a clean title filter applied at Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: Same E46-era BMW oil leak checkpoints apply: valve cover, rear main, oil pan. Z3 soft tops are simple manual affairs — check the header latches and rear window condition. Look at hood-to-fender gaps for any prior accident repair. Verify top drain tubes aren’t clogged (leads to wet footwells). Confirm which engine variant (2.3 vs refreshed 2.5 — varies mid-2000 production).
picks.bottomLine: A BMW roadster at 75K miles under $11K with Good Deal — a solid, fun convertible for regular weekend use.

picks.title: 2003 Mercedes-Benz SLK 230 (95K mi)
picks.location: Private Seller, ~33 mi (via AutoTrader Private Seller Exchange)
picks.whyGood: The R170 SLK 230 Kompressor is mechanically simpler and more reliable than later SLK V6/V8 siblings. No accidents reported and a price drop to $6,730. The big appeal is the Vario-Roof retractable hardtop — no aging fabric top to worry about. The supercharged 2.3L inline-4 is low-stress to maintain.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the Vario-Roof through full open and close several times — slow or hesitant operation means low hydraulic fluid or a worn pump, and repairs are expensive. Check under the rear deck for fluid weeps. Inspect the supercharger belt and intercooler. Common SLK issues: front lower ball joints, window regulators, and airflow meter. Private seller — verify clean title and ask for all maintenance records.
picks.bottomLine: A retractable hardtop roadster for $6,730 with no accidents — great value if the roof mechanism passes inspection.

picks.title: 2003 Mercedes-Benz CLK 320 Cabriolet (101K mi)
picks.location: DIRECT AUTO SALES, Philadelphia PA (24 mi)
picks.whyGood: The CLK 320 Cabriolet is the most refined car on this list — a four-seat full soft-top Mercedes with a 3.2L V6 that purrs. At 101K miles it’s the freshest CLK on the board. For under $6K this is exceptional value: the CLK 320 is far simpler and cheaper to maintain than the SL500 V8.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top should fold and raise without hesitation — check the rear window for scratches or delamination. Inspect for oil leaks at the camshaft cover and transmission pan (722.6 auto commonly seeps). Check convertible top latch alignment. Listen for clunking over bumps (control arm bushings). Verify floor drain points aren’t clogged — CLK cabrios can hold water if drains fail.
picks.bottomLine: A four-seat Mercedes cabriolet at $5,889 with 101K miles — excellent value if the top operates clean and no hidden rust.

picks.title: 2008 Audi A4 3.2 quattro Cabriolet AWD
picks.location: Selden Motors, Willow Grove PA (23 mi)
picks.whyGood: The B7 A4 3.2 Cabriolet is the sport-touring pick — quattro AWD, naturally-aspirated 3.2L V6, proper soft-top body. CarGurus Great Deal at $5,472 from Selden Motors in Willow Grove. The 3.2 avoids turbo complexity of the 2.0T.
picks.whatToVerify: The 3.2 FSI has well-documented carbon buildup on intake valves — factor in walnut blasting if you buy. Check timing chain tensioners (known B7 FSI weak point). Cycle the soft top several times. Inspect for oil leaks. At 127K miles an independent Audi specialist pre-purchase inspection is strongly recommended before committing.
picks.bottomLine: Best-priced Audi cabriolet on the board with Great Deal rating — go in with eyes open on the 3.2 FSI’s known maintenance items.

picks.title: 2011 Volvo C70 T5
picks.location: JC Miller Auto Sales, Vineland NJ (35 mi)
picks.whyGood: The C70 T5 is the hidden gem of the convertible world — a retractable hardtop (no aging fabric) that folds silently, genuinely safe Volvo build quality, and the 2.5T five-cylinder is entertaining without being fragile. At 93K miles and Good Deal from Cars.com it’s priced fairly. Hardtop means all-weather usability no soft top can match.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the retractable hardtop slowly and listen for any binding or motor strain — the folding mechanism is complex and repairs are expensive if the alignment drifts. Check all three roof panel gap alignments. Turbo boost should be strong and clean with no smoke. Verify timing belt service history (Volvo five-cylinder needs it around 105K intervals). Vineland is 35 miles — call ahead before making the trip.
picks.bottomLine: Safe, all-weather retractable hardtop at a fair price — ideal if you want comfort and reliability over raw sport.

picks.title: 2004 Mercedes-Benz SL 500 Roadster
picks.whyGood: The R230 SL500 at 53K miles is a remarkable low-mileage listing at $11,989. It’s the most grand-touring car on the list — retractable hardtop, V8, and presence. Great Deal badge from Cars.com. CarsIndex Warminster is 25 miles away. The M113 V8 itself is essentially bulletproof.
picks.whatToVerify: The ABC (Active Body Control) hydraulic suspension is the SL500’s biggest wildcard — if failing, the car sags on one corner and ABC warning lights appear; repair costs are significant. Inspect the ABC pump, lines, and accumulators carefully. The retractable hardtop is also ABC-actuated and must cycle fully and cleanly. Ask specifically for service records mentioning ABC fluid changes.
picks.bottomLine: 53K miles and Great Deal on a V8 retractable-hardtop roadster — spectacular buy if the ABC suspension checks clean; walk away if it doesn’t.
